docs.title: Civic Accountability Toolkit
docs.text: An evidence-first toolkit designed to empower citizens to organize public facts, track incidents with reliable sources, and send fact-based oversight requests to their representatives.

docs.title: Why This Exists
docs.text: In a complex political landscape, it is crucial to separate documented facts from allegations and commentary. This toolkit provides a structured, neutral framework for: - Tracking actions and statements made by public figures. - Linking every claim directly to verified public records or reliable reporting. - Empowering ordinary citizens to write informed, respectful, and effective letters to oversight committees and elected officials.

docs.title: How to Get Started
docs.text:
The best way to use this toolkit is as a local knowledge base.

  1. Clone or Download: Clone this repository to your local machine.
  2. Open the Vault: For the best experience, download Obsidian (a free, Markdown-based note-taking app) and open the civic-accountability folder as a new vault.
  3. Start Exploring: Open the 00_HOME.md file to see the main dashboard, or browse the people/ and incidents/ folders to read documented cases.
  4. Review the Evidence: Each incident links directly to sources in the evidence/ folder. Review these to understand what is documented versus what remains unresolved.
  5. Take Action: Use the templates/citizen-letter-template.md to draft a neutral, fact-based oversight request and send it to your Representative, Senators, or the relevant oversight office.

docs.title: Core Principles
docs.text: We hold ourselves to a high standard of civic participation: - Evidence-First: Link every single claim to a verifiable source note. - Remain Neutral: Separate what happened (documented facts) from what may apply legally and what still needs proof. - Presumption of Innocence: Do not state guilt unless there is a final legal finding or conviction. - Respectful Outreach: Use official channels and maintain a professional, non-defamatory tone.

docs.title: Safe Civic Use
docs.text:
This repository is designed strictly for lawful civic participation, public-record review, and oversight requests.

Not Allowed: - Threats, harassment, or doxxing. - Fabricated claims or presenting allegations as convictions. - Spam or abusive outreach campaigns.

For detailed documentation, contribution guidelines, and advanced workflows, please visit our Documentation Site or browse the docs/ folder.

docs.title: One-line summary
docs.text: Termination of the "Affirmatively Furthering Fair Housing" (AFFH) rule by HUD in 2025.

docs.title: What is documented
docs.text: HUD Secretary Scott Turner signed the directive terminating the AFFH rule in 2025, which required local governments to address patterns of housing discrimination and segregation.

docs.title: What remains alleged or unresolved
docs.text: Litigants argue the repeal violates the Fair Housing Act of 1968, which mandates that the executive branch proactively work to eliminate housing discrimination.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: The repeal removes a primary mechanism for federal enforcement of fair housing standards at the municipal level.

docs.title: Suggested oversight ask
docs.text: Request the Office of Legal Counsel (OLC) opinion regarding HUD's statutory obligation to "affirmatively further" fair housing following the rule's repeal.

docs.title: One-line summary
docs.text: Mass termination and forced retirement of FBI agents and officials perceived as "insufficiently loyal" following Kash Patel's appointment as Director.

docs.title: What is documented
docs.text: Since early 2025, Director Kash Patel has overseen the removal of dozens of career FBI agents, including many who worked on investigations involving Donald Trump. The administration describes this as "clearing the deep state."

docs.title: What remains alleged or unresolved
docs.text: Fired agents allege the terminations were retaliatory and violated civil service protections. A class-action lawsuit (Agents v. FBI) was filed in March 2026.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: The independence of the FBI and the protection of career civil servants from political retribution are central to public integrity.

docs.title: Suggested oversight ask
docs.text: Request all personnel records and internal emails regarding the "loyalty-based" termination criteria used by the Office of the Director since January 2025.

docs.title: One-line summary
docs.text: 25% reduction of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI) workforce in 2025 as part of administration-wide downsizing.

docs.title: What is documented
docs.text: Director Tulsi Gabbard, in collaboration with the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), implemented a 25% staff reduction at the ODNI during 2025.

docs.title: What remains alleged or unresolved
docs.text: Intelligence professionals have raised concerns regarding the impact on national security readiness and whether the layoffs targeted specific analytic units for political reasons.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: Massive reductions in the intelligence community's administrative and analytic staff can impact the quality and timeliness of national threat assessments.

docs.title: Suggested oversight ask
docs.text: Request a breakdown of the specific roles and divisions impacted by the 2025 ODNI layoffs and a risk assessment of the resulting loss in human intelligence capabilities.

docs.title: One-line summary
docs.text: Allegations that high-ranking officials shared classified military data via encrypted Signal chats with unauthorized parties.

docs.title: What is documented
docs.text: House Oversight Committee testimony in April 2026 identified an encrypted group chat containing JD Vance, Pete Hegseth, and others. Digital forensic snapshots provided by whistleblowers show the sharing of coordinates for Yemen airstrikes prior to official declassification.

docs.title: What remains alleged or unresolved
docs.text: The extent of the distribution and whether the intent was for "leaking" or internal administrative convenience (which still violates handling protocols).

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: Violation of 18 U.S.C. § 793 (Espionage Act) protocols regarding national defense information.

docs.title: Suggested oversight ask
docs.text: Request a formal damage assessment from the DNI and a referral to the DOJ Public Integrity Section for unauthorized disclosure of classified information.

docs.title: One-line summary
docs.text: 2025 removal of 11,000 commercial drivers from the workforce based on new English proficiency enforcement standards.

docs.title: What is documented
docs.text: Under Secretary Sean Duffy, the Department of Transportation removed over 11,000 drivers and shut down 7,500 CDL training schools in 2025, citing a strict interpretation of English language requirements.

docs.title: What remains alleged or unresolved
docs.text: Labor groups contend the purge disproportionately targeted immigrant communities and violated due process by revoking existing valid licenses without individual hearings.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: This impacts the national supply chain and raises significant civil rights and due process concerns regarding occupational licensing.

docs.title: Suggested oversight ask
docs.text: Request the legal basis for the retroactive revocation of CDL licenses and an impact assessment on the national trucking workforce.

docs.title: One-line summary
docs.text: Proposed April 2026 executive order requiring U.S. banks to report the citizenship status of all account holders to the Treasury.

docs.title: What is documented
docs.text: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced in April 2026 that an executive order is "in process" that would mandate banks to collect and verify the citizenship status of all customers for reporting to the federal government.

docs.title: What remains alleged or unresolved
docs.text: Privacy advocates and banking associations argue this violates the Privacy Act and the 4th Amendment, creating a "financial surveillance state" without a clear statutory mandate.

docs.title: Suggested oversight ask

docs.title: One-line summary
docs.text: February 2026 repeal of the 2009 EPA Endangerment Finding, removing the legal basis for federal greenhouse gas regulation.

docs.title: What is documented
docs.text: EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin signed the final rule in February 2026. This action eliminates the requirement for the EPA to regulate carbon emissions under the Clean Air Act by asserting that greenhouse gases do not "endanger" public health.

docs.title: What remains alleged or unresolved
docs.text: A coalition of 25 states alleges the EPA "fabricated" or cherry-picked scientific data to reach a pre-determined political outcome, violating the Administrative Procedure Act.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: This is a foundational shift in environmental law that affects federal authority over vehicle emissions and industrial pollutants.

docs.title: Suggested oversight ask
docs.text: Request an Inspector General audit of the peer-review process for the scientific studies cited in the February 2026 repeal.

docs.title: One-line summary
docs.text: March 2026 appointment of Vice President JD Vance to lead a federal task force targeting alleged administrative fraud in "blue states."

docs.title: What is documented
docs.text: Vice President JD Vance was appointed in March 2026 to lead the "Task Force to Eliminate Fraud." The task force is directed to audit state-level administrative records, specifically focusing on jurisdictions that have identified as "sanctuary" or non-compliant with certain federal directives.

docs.title: What remains alleged or unresolved
docs.text: Legal experts and state officials contend this task force violates the 10th Amendment (State Sovereignty) and the Privacy Act, as it seeks to compel the sharing of state-held sensitive data without traditional judicial oversight.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: This represents a potential expansion of federal oversight into state-level administration and benefit distribution.

docs.title: Suggested oversight ask
docs.text: Request the charter and jurisdictional legal assessment for the Task Force to Eliminate Fraud and a list of all state data requests issued by the Vice President's office.

docs.title: One-line summary
docs.text: February 2026 order by CIA Director John Ratcliffe to retract and revise previous intelligence assessments deemed "politically biased."

docs.title: What is documented
docs.text: In February 2026, Director Ratcliffe issued a directive to the Directorate of Intelligence to retract several high-profile assessments, including those related to domestic extremism and foreign interference, asserting they failed to meet tradecraft standards.

docs.title: What remains alleged or unresolved
docs.text: Whistleblowers and former analysts allege the retractions were politically motivated to remove assessments that were critical of the administration's allies or policy positions.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: The integrity of intelligence analysis is mandated by the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act (IRTPA). Politicization of intelligence can lead to skewed policy-making.

docs.title: Suggested oversight ask
docs.text: Request all internal CIA evaluations that supposedly identified the "analytic tradecraft failures" leading to the February 2026 retractions.

docs.title: One-line summary
docs.text: Congressional and expert criticism of the administration’s Iran campaign raised war-powers and international-law concerns.

docs.title: What is documented
docs.text: Reporting describes congressional objections to the campaign and expert warnings about legality and possible laws-of-war concerns.

docs.title: What remains alleged or unresolved
docs.text: Whether any action was unlawful or criminal remains contested and would require far more than public reporting to establish.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: This is suitable for war-powers oversight requests and record preservation demands.

docs.title: Suggested oversight ask
docs.text: Request classified and unclassified briefings, preservation of legal memos, and review by relevant oversight bodies.

docs.text:
• Role: Secretary of Defense
  • Group: Cabinet
  • Office status: current

docs.title: 1. Unauthorized War (Operation Epic Fury)
docs.text:
• Action: Authorized strikes on Iran in Feb 2026 without a new Congressional AUMF.
  • Alleged Violation: War Powers Resolution of 1973.
  • Status: Subject to 6 Articles of Impeachment (April 2026).
  • Link: incidents/2026-iran-war-powers-dispute

docs.title: 2. First Amendment (Press Restrictions)
docs.text:
• Action: Late 2025 imposition of new Pentagon press rules barring major news outlets.
  • Alleged Violation: 1st Amendment (Freedom of the Press).
  • Status: Litigation active (NYT v. DoD).
  • Link: incidents/2025-pentagon-press-restrictions

docs.title: Linked Statutes
docs.text:
• statutes/war-powers-framework
  • statutes/first-amendment

docs.title: Suggested Oversight Ask
docs.text: Request all civilian casualty assessments related to the Feb 2026 Iran strikes and the internal legal justification for the 2025 press access restrictions.

docs.title: Snapshot
docs.text:
• Role: Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS)
  • Group: Cabinet
  • Office status: current

docs.title: 1. HHS Workforce Purge & Reorganization
docs.text:
• Action: March 2025. Announced plan to lay off 10,000 employees and collapse 28 agencies into 15.
  • Alleged Violation: Violation of civil service laws and statutory requirements for agency structure.
  • Status: Partially blocked by preliminary injunction (April 2025).
  • Link: incidents/2025-hhs-workforce-purge

docs.title: 2. Vaccine Schedule Revision (Administrative Procedure Act)
docs.text:
• Action: February 2026. Unilateral revision of the national vaccine schedule without public comment periods.
  • Alleged Violation: Administrative Procedure Act (APA) violations.
  • Status: Litigation active (14 States v. HHS, Feb 2026).
  • Link: incidents/2026-vaccine-schedule-litigation

docs.title: Linked Statutes
docs.text:
• statutes/public-integrity-statutes
  • statutes/fifth-amendment-due-process

docs.title: Suggested Oversight Ask
docs.text: Request all internal memos regarding the decision to bypass APA public comment requirements for the 2026 vaccine schedule revisions and a breakdown of the 10,000 positions targeted for removal.

docs.title: Snapshot
docs.text:
• Role: Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB)
  • Status: De facto head of DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency).

docs.title: 1. "Blue State" Funding Freezes
docs.text:
• Action: 2025-2026. Frozen or canceled billions in authorized funding for climate projects and infrastructure in specific states (e.g., $18B for NYC).
  • Alleged Violation: Impoundment Control Act of 1974.
  • Status: Subject to multiple state-level lawsuits.
  • Link: incidents/2025-2026-funding-freezes-blue-states

docs.title: 2. Sidelining the GAO
docs.text:
• Action: Issued guidance in late 2025 to federal agencies to ignore Government Accountability Office (GAO) inquiries and oversight.
  • Alleged Violation: Violation of statutory oversight mandates for the GAO.
  • Status: Active dispute with Congress.
  • Link: incidents/2025-impoundment-control-dispute

docs.title: Linked Statutes
docs.text:
• statutes/public-integrity-statutes
  • statutes/war-powers-framework (Budgetary context)

docs.title: Suggested Oversight Ask
docs.text: Request all OLC memos regarding the legal authority to withhold congressionally appropriated funds for specific states based on "financial governance" criteria.

docs.title: Snapshot
docs.text:
• Role: Secretary of the Treasury
  • Group: Cabinet
  • Office status: current

docs.title: 1. Bank Citizenship Reporting Order
docs.text:
• Action: April 2026 announcement of a pending executive order requiring all U.S. banks to collect and report the citizenship status of account holders to the Treasury.
  • Alleged Violation: 4th Amendment (Privacy/Search), Potential violation of the Privacy Act.
  • Status: Directive in development.
  • Link: incidents/2026-bank-citizenship-reporting-order

docs.title: 2. EITC Exclusion for Non-Citizens
docs.text:
• Action: November 2025 reclassification of tax credits (EITC) as "federal public benefits" to prevent claims by undocumented residents.
  • Alleged Violation: Statutory interpretation concerns regarding the Internal Revenue Code and equal protection principles.
  • Status: Implemented.

docs.title: Linked Statutes
docs.text:
• statutes/fourteenth-amendment
  • statutes/public-integrity-statutes

docs.title: Suggested Oversight Ask
docs.text: Request the legal analysis for the bank citizenship reporting requirement and an evaluation of its impact on the privacy rights of legal residents and U.S. citizens.

docs.title: Snapshot
docs.text:
• Role: Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)
  • Group: Cabinet
  • Office status: current

docs.title: 1. Homelessness Grant Competitive Restructuring
docs.text:
• Action: 2025. Announced $3.9 billion in grants with a new requirement that 70% of projects be competitively bid, shifting away from long-term "Housing First" providers.
  • Alleged Violation: Administrative Procedure Act (APA) concerns regarding the sudden change in grant eligibility and "self-sufficiency" criteria.
  • Status: Implementation active.
  • Link: incidents/2025-homelessness-grant-restructuring

docs.title: 2. Repeal of Affirmatively Furthering Fair Housing (AFFH)
docs.text:
• Action: 2025. Terminated the AFFH rule which required local governments to proactively address segregation.
  • Alleged Violation: Fair Housing Act (FHA) statutory mandates.
  • Status: Subject to civil rights litigation.
  • Link: incidents/2025-affh-rule-repeal

docs.title: Linked Statutes
docs.text:
• statutes/public-integrity-statutes
  • statutes/fifth-amendment-due-process

docs.title: Suggested Oversight Ask
docs.text: Request the legal justification for the termination of the AFFH rule and an IG review of the selection criteria for the new transitional housing grant awards.

docs.title: Snapshot
docs.text:
• Role: Secretary of Transportation
  • Group: Cabinet
  • Office status: current

docs.title: 1. Trucking Industry "English Purge"
docs.text:
• Action: 2025 removal of 11,000 drivers from the workforce for failing English proficiency standards and shutting down 7,500 CDL training schools.
  • Alleged Violation: Potential violation of civil rights statutes regarding national origin discrimination and due process in license revocations.
  • Status: Implemented.
  • Link: incidents/2025-trucking-language-purge

docs.title: 2. Infrastructure Funding Redirection
docs.text:
• Action: Redirected billions in funding away from previously approved "green" infrastructure (California high-speed rail, offshore wind) toward traditional road capacity.
  • Alleged Violation: Potential violation of the Impoundment Control Act and statutory funding mandates.
  • Status: Ongoing.
  • Link: incidents/2026-infrastructure-funding-redirection

docs.title: Linked Statutes
docs.text:
• statutes/public-integrity-statutes
  • statutes/fifth-amendment-due-process

docs.title: Suggested Oversight Ask
docs.text: Request the legal basis for the mass revocation of CDL licenses based on English proficiency and a breakdown of the redirected "green" infrastructure funds.

docs.title: Snapshot
docs.text:
• Role: Director of National Intelligence (DNI)
  • Group: Intelligence
  • Office status: current

docs.title: 1. ODNI Workforce Reduction (DOGE Collaboration)
docs.text:
• Action: 2025 reduction of the ODNI workforce by 25%.
  • Alleged Violation: Potential violation of civil service protections and statutory staffing mandates for national security operations.
  • Status: Implemented.
  • Link: incidents/2025-odni-workforce-reduction

docs.title: 2. Criminal Referrals (Impeachment Retaliation Allegations)
docs.text:
• Action: April 16, 2026. Criminal referrals sent to the DOJ targeting the former IC Inspector General and the 2019 whistleblower.
  • Alleged Violation: 1st Amendment (Retaliation), Violation of Whistleblower Protection Act (WPA) standards.
  • Status: Referrals sent.
  • Link: incidents/2026-intelligence-criminal-referrals

docs.title: Linked Statutes
docs.text:
• statutes/public-integrity-statutes
  • statutes/first-amendment

docs.title: Suggested Oversight Ask
docs.text: Request all documentation justifying the 2026 criminal referrals and an evaluation from the DOJ Public Integrity Section on whether these referrals comply with the Whistleblower Protection Act.

docs.title: Snapshot
docs.text:
• Role: Second Lady of the United States
  • Group: First Family
  • Office status: current

docs.title: 1. Literacy and Education Platforms
docs.text:
• Action: Launched the "Summer Reading Challenge" (2025) and the "Storytime with the Second Lady" podcast (2026) to address pandemic-era reading loss in children.
  • Link: incidents/2026-storytime-podcast-launch

docs.title: 2. Special Olympics Advocacy
docs.text: • Action: Increased federal visibility and involvement with the Special Olympics as a primary ceremonial platform.

docs.title: Suggested Oversight Ask
docs.text: Review the Department of Education's coordination with the Office of the Second Lady on the distribution of "Summer Reading" grants.

docs.title: Establishment Clause note
docs.text: Government actions relating to religion may raise constitutional issues if they advance, favor, or entangle government with religion in unlawful ways.

docs.title: Plain-English note
docs.text: Protects due process against federal government action.

docs.title: In this repo
docs.text: Used where federal action allegedly imposed penalties or adverse action without fair procedures.

docs.title: Plain-English note
docs.text: Protects speech, press, religion, assembly, and petition.

docs.title: In this repo
docs.text: Used for incident mapping where official actions may burden speech, press access, or retaliation concerns.

docs.title: Do not overclaim
docs.text: A policy dispute is not automatically a First Amendment violation; facts and court rulings matter.

docs.title: Plain-English note
docs.text: Includes the Citizenship Clause and equal protection / due process guarantees.

docs.title: In this repo
docs.text: Used mainly for the birthright citizenship litigation mapping.

docs.title: Immigration due process note
docs.text: Administrative and constitutional protections can apply in immigration adjudication and status determinations.

docs.title: War Crimes Act note
docs.text: This note is for very high-threshold allegations only. Public reporting alone is not enough to conclude criminal war-crimes liability.

docs.title: Plain-English note
docs.text: The Constitution and federal war-powers framework allocate responsibilities between Congress and the President concerning the use of force.

docs.title: In this repo
docs.text: Used to frame oversight questions about authorization, reporting, and legal basis.
